projectDescription: An autonomous AI research framework that runs LLM pretraining experiments on a single GPU overnight, iterating on model architecture and hyperparameters to minimize validation bits-per-byte.
keyFeatures: Autonomous overnight experimentation — agent modifies train.py, trains for 5 minutes, evaluates, and iterates without human intervention
Fixed 5-minute time budget per experiment enabling fair comparison across architectures and hyperparameters
Vocabulary-size-independent val_bpb metric for evaluating across diverse model configurations
Single-file model: GPT with RoPE, RMSNorm, Flash Attention 3, sliding window attention pattern (SSSL), and value embeddings (ResFormer)
MuonAdamW optimizer combining Muon (matrix params) and AdamW (embeddings/scalars) with torch.compile fused kernels
Human-programmable research org via program.md — the 'research org code' that guides agent behavior and strategy
Self-contained single-GPU setup using uv for reproducible dependency management
publicApiSurface: uv run prepare.py — one-time data download and BPE tokenizer training
uv run train.py — single training experiment (5-minute fixed time budget)
GPTConfig dataclass — model architecture configuration (sequence_len, vocab_size, n_layer, n_head, n_kv_head, n_embd, window_pattern)
GPT.setup_optimizer() — constructs MuonAdamW optimizer with per-group learning rates
GPT.forward(idx, targets, reduction) — forward pass with optional cross-entropy loss
evaluate_bpb(model, tokenizer, batch_size) — fixed validation bits-per-byte metric
make_dataloader(tokenizer, B, T, split) — BOS-aligned best-fit packing dataloader
Hyperparameter constants: DEPTH, ASPECT_RATIO, HEAD_DIM, WINDOW_PATTERN, TOTAL_BATCH_SIZE, EMBEDDING_LR, MATRIX_LR, WARMDOWN_RATIO
